title: [InstCombine] Recognize non-negative subtraction patterns
author: user1342234
description:
Alive2 proofs:
Select pattern: https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/YjBg-S
smin pattern: https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/-E2Tpc

Fixes #146131

comments.author: user1342234
comments.body:
> InstCombine converts `sext` to `zext nneg` when `isKnownNonNegative(Src)` returns true:
> 
> https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/blob/1aaa33883b0407d9e427bc105d4014b3a9ae8801/llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineCasts.cpp#L1765-L1769
> 
> In `computeKnownBitsAddSub`, we can infer `sub nsw` is non-negative if `icmp sle op1, op0` is implied by the dominating condition:
> 
> https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/blob/af9ca0e5bea319a976e57cf92d6aa033ffce44b2/llvm/lib/Analysis/ValueTracking.cpp#L501-L504
> 
> I'd suggest matching the pattern in `computeKnownBitsAddSub`, as it benefits all computeKnownBits users. Although we have handled `icmp sle smin(x, y), x -> true` in `simplifyICmpWithMinMax`, it cannot be reused because ValueTracking cannot depend on InstructionSimplify. We can leave a todo to move the folding logic into ValueTracking.

Hello! Matching the pattern for `smin` works fine  inside `computeKnownBitsAddSub` , but I would have to add logic in `computeKnownBitsFromOperator` at the `Instruction::Select` case to handle when `select` is used. Is this ok? It seems a little messy as the logic for folding intrinsics are split up in different places: #177568

Perhaps I could fold `select` to smin/smax in these cases? I can do this in `InstCombineSelect.cpp` `foldSelectICmpMinMax`. Please let me know!

comments.author: dtcxzyw
comments.body:
> but I would have to add logic in computeKnownBitsFromOperator at the Instruction::Select case to handle when select is used. Is this ok?

You can investigate why `adjustKnownBitsForSelectArm -> computeKnownBitsFromCond` fails to infer the false value of the select is non-negative.

> Perhaps I could fold select to smin/smax in these cases? I can do this in InstCombineSelect.cpp foldSelectICmpMinMax. Please let me know!

Yeah, it seems like a reasonable canonicalization: https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/Zt-c2m

comments.author: user1342234
comments.body:
Hi! I've created patterns in `ValueTracking.cpp` at `computeKnownBitsAddSub` and folded specific cases of `ICmp` to use `smin` intrinsics in `InstCombineSelect.cpp` `foldSelectICmpMinMax`. As such, I've updated the tests to reflect these changes.

I would also have to do this for `smax`. Is it okay for me to make a seperate issue for that and take it?


 Note: I deleted some of the original commits by accident. It was just logic inside of `InstCombineCasts.cpp` but I believe the test file survived. If I need to redo the PR please let me know.
